# jackknife

Auto-generated by jackknife.

- [architecture] Blade pattern: each capability is a self-contained module with base.py (Protocol + ABC), models.py (Pydantic), factory.py (wiring), and one or more implementation files. All blades are optional extras in pyproject.toml.
- [architecture] Exception hierarchy: all exceptions inherit from JackknifeError. Blade-specific: LLMError, StorageError, MemoryError, DataConnectorError, MCPError, AgentError, ScaffoldError. Always raise the most specific subclass.
- [architecture] Task graph orchestration: NetworkX DiGraph where edges A→B mean 'B depends on A'. ready_tasks() returns PENDING tasks with all deps COMPLETED. Orchestrator runs up to max_parallel tasks concurrently with asyncio.gather().
- [architecture] Config via pydantic-settings. Each blade has its own *Settings class. Top-level Settings composes all blades. get_settings() is @lru_cache — call get_settings.cache_clear() in tests. All path fields must be absolute.
- [architecture] Memory write queue: MemoryWriteQueue serializes concurrent writes to ChromaDB via asyncio.Queue. Multiple agents can enqueue concurrently; writes processed one at a time. Required because ChromaDB is not thread-safe.
- [architecture] LiteLLM is the only LLM provider implementation. Model string selects provider: gpt-4o-mini→OpenAI, claude-3-5-sonnet-latest→Anthropic, gemini/gemini-2.0-flash→Google, ollama/llama3.2→Ollama. API keys via env vars.
- [architecture] All async I/O: sync third-party SDKs (ChromaDB, boto3, GCS, Azure, pandas, neo4j) are wrapped with asyncio.get_running_loop().run_in_executor(None, partial(fn, *args)). Native async SDKs (motor, redis.asyncio, SQLAlchemy asyncio, neo4j AsyncDriver) used directly.
- [convention] Commit message format (commitizen enforced): feat:, fix:, docs:, refactor:, test:, chore:, ci:. Never commit to main directly. Always branch → commit → PR.
- [convention] CI/CD: ruff (lint+format), mypy strict, pytest with 80% coverage required. Pre-commit runs all three locally. GitHub Actions (test.yml) runs on every push/PR. publish.yml uses PyPI Trusted Publishing (OIDC) on version tags.
- [convention] Use Poetry for all dependency management. Never use pip or python3 directly. Always: poetry run <cmd>.
